task:
Create a durable, one-command red→green proof harness for the orchestration-contract enforcer (Objective §4 of the Single-source orchestration contracts priority). This proves the structural enforcer actually catches a re-encoded contract, rather than relying on a reviewer to notice.

WHAT TO BUILD
Add a new file `scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs`, following the conventions of the existing sibling harnesses in `scripts/proof-*.mjs` (Node ESM, runnable via `node scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs`, exit 0 = proof passed, non-zero with a clear message = proof failed, concise PASS/FAIL summary printed). Read at least one sibling (e.g. `scripts/proof-direct-spine.mjs` or `scripts/proof-governance-authoring.mjs`) first and match its style for shelling out and reporting.

The enforcer under proof is the test `packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts`, specifically its first test: `live prompt/runtime/test consumers do not restate the founder closeout contract`. That test derives the founder-closeout section labels from the fenced contract in `packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md` (the single owner) and fails if any live consumer file restates >=3 of those section labels.

The harness must perform a green→red→green proof:
1. GREEN baseline: run the enforcer test on the clean tree and assert it PASSES.
2. RED: programmatically introduce a deliberate duplicate of the founder-closeout contract — inject >=3 of the wrap-up Play's fenced `**Section**` labels into ONE live consumer listed in the test (e.g. append them as a comment block to `packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts`). Re-run the enforcer test and assert it now FAILS, and that the failing test is the founder-closeout-restatement test (not an unrelated failure).
3. GREEN again: restore the consumer file to its exact original bytes, re-run the enforcer test, and assert it PASSES again.

HARD REQUIREMENTS
- Capture the original file content in memory before mutating; restore it in a `try/finally` so the file is ALWAYS restored even if a step throws. After the run the working tree must be byte-identical to before, except for the new `scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` file itself. Verify by reading the file back and comparing.
- Derive the injected labels from the wrap-up Play at runtime (parse its fenced contract), so the harness stays correct if the contract's labels change — do NOT hard-code the label strings (that would itself be a second copy of the contract, the exact anti-pattern this priority forbids).
- Do NOT weaken, edit, or skip `orchestration-contracts.test.ts` or any other test. Do NOT run git. Do NOT modify any committed source other than adding the new script.
- To run the single test file, use the repo's standard vitest invocation for the core package (inspect `packages/core/package.json` scripts and the sibling proof harnesses to get the exact command; e.g. a `pnpm -F @cocoder/core exec vitest run tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts` style call). Parse the child process exit code to determine pass/fail.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `node scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` exits 0 and prints a summary showing the enforcer went GREEN (baseline) → RED (duplicate injected) → GREEN (restored).
- Running it leaves no residual working-tree changes (only the new script remains as an addition); confirm and report `git status --porcelain` output after a run.
- The full core test suite still passes (run it and report the count).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: the existing core suite (was 379/379 green), the enforcer test, and the clean working tree. Report the exact commands you ran and their output as evidence.

Atom 0 was rejected for scope: the only thing this atom may land is the new proof harness `scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs`. Your previous run also made many undelegated out-of-scope edits that must NOT be committed. This atom is to RESTORE the tree to a clean state so the only change is the harness addition.

DO EXACTLY THIS — no more, no less.

1. Restore these tracked files to their committed HEAD state (use `git restore <path>` or `git checkout HEAD -- <path>`; this is a working-tree restore, NOT a commit/branch/push — that is allowed):
   - cocoder/AGENTS.md
   - cocoder/failure-catalog.md
   - cocoder/priorities/founder-brief-format-durability.md
   - cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md
   - docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md
   - packages/personas/base/bob.md
   - packages/personas/base/oscar.md
   - packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md
   - packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts

2. Restore these files you DELETED back to their committed HEAD content (same `git restore`/`git checkout HEAD --`):
   - cocoder/priorities/founder-brief-format-durability.owner-inventory.md
   - cocoder/tickets/open/0005-persona-file-memory-migrations.md

3. Delete these untracked files you created (they are out of scope for this atom and will be redone later under proper scope):
   - cocoder/personas/deltas/oscar.md
   - cocoder/tickets/closed/0005-persona-file-memory-migrations.md
   - cocoder/tickets/open/0018-agent-commit-gate-bypass.md

4. DO NOT touch these — they are runner bookkeeping, not yours: `cocoder/counters.json` and anything under `cocoder/runs/`. Leave them exactly as they are.

5. KEEP `scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` exactly as it is (it is correct and is the only thing this atom lands). Do NOT edit it.

Then verify the harness is intact and the tree is clean:
   - Run `node scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` and confirm it prints GREEN -> RED -> GREEN and exits 0.
   - Run the full core test suite and the personas test suite; confirm they pass (report the counts).
   - Run `git status --porcelain` and confirm the ONLY non-bookkeeping entry is `?? scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` (entries for `cocoder/counters.json` and `cocoder/runs/...` are expected and fine; nothing else may appear).

ACCEPTANCE: `git status --porcelain` shows the harness as the sole added file (ignoring counters.json and runs/), every restored/deleted file above is back to or removed per the list, the harness still proves GREEN->RED->GREEN with exit 0, and both test suites are green. Report the exact commands and their output as evidence.

Apply ONLY the PORTABLE parts of ticket cocoder/tickets/open/0005-persona-file-memory-migrations.md (items 3, 4, 5) to the governed base persona/standards files. This is the package/persona migration atom. SCOPE IS HARD: you may edit ONLY files under `packages/personas/**`. Do NOT touch cocoder/**, docs/**, or any other package. Do NOT create cocoder/personas/deltas/oscar.md and do NOT edit cocoder/AGENTS.md — items 1 and 2 are repo-specific and are handled in a separate Oscar-scoped atom, NOT here. Read ticket 0005 for the exact rule wording.

APPLY THESE THREE RULES (keep each PORTABLE — ADR-0012 test: no repo-specific nouns like daemon/run/cmux/Oz/CoCoder; phrase so it still teaches the role with repo nouns stripped):

- Item 3 -> `packages/personas/base/oscar.md`: add an 'adversarial plan review' rule — before a substantial build or refactor, offer (or run) a focused adversarial review of the plan against the project's own decisions and failure history; it repeatedly catches real blockers before code. Reserve heavy multi-agent workflows for review/verification, never for building a deliberately thin spine.

- Item 4 -> `packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md` (Communication And Judgment section): add a 'design-seam discussion style' rule — on consequential or nuanced architecture seams, lead with honest prose reasoning plus ONE focused open question; reserve multiple-choice for genuinely crisp, bounded picks; when the founder pushes back on a framing, suspect the QUESTION is wrong, not just the options.

- Item 5 -> `packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md` (Evidence section) and/or `packages/personas/base/bob.md` (Completion evidence): add 'launchability + green-claims' rules — (i) build + typecheck + unit tests can all be green while the app cannot launch; only a real launch smoke (bounded watchdog, reject-if-loaded-artifact-missing, never an infinite poll) proves launchability — never infer launchability from build/test success; (ii) never report a build/smoke/generated artifact green without reading the actual command exit code and a real (path/size/timestamp) artifact, and keep verification batches small so one cancellation cannot silently skip checks.

TEST PINS: update `packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts` to add a minimal assertion per new rule that pins it to its governed file (the rule's presence in oscar.md / shared-standards.md / bob.md). Do NOT copy any closeout-contract labels and do NOT restate the founder-closeout format anywhere (the orchestration-contracts enforcer must stay green).

EVIDENCE (run and report exact commands + output): the personas test suite (`packages/personas` vitest) green, and a typecheck green. Also confirm the orchestration-contracts enforcer test still passes.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: orchestration-contracts.test.ts and the full personas/core suites stay green; no files outside packages/personas/** are modified (confirm with `git status --porcelain`). Do NOT run git commit/push/branch — the runner commits on my verify pass.
